title: 怪钟疑案
summary:
Chief Inspector Hakast interrogates Mrs. Rivard regarding a man in a newspaper photograph. Mrs. Rivard identifies the man as her husband, Harry Castleman, whom she claims to have married on May 15, 1948. She asserts that Harry possesses a distinctive scar behind his left ear, resulting from a razor accident involving a dog in late 1948, and maintains she has not seen him since she sent him away in 1951.

Hakast challenges this testimony, noting that forensic examinations indicate the scar tissue is only five or six years old. He argues that if the scar is recent, the man cannot be Harry, as Harry did not have the mark when he left her in 1951. Hakast warns Mrs. Rivard that providing false information could lead to perjury charges. While she initially reacts with indignant defiance, claiming she is merely performing her civic duty, her demeanor shifts to fear and anxiety after Hakast leaves.

Mrs. Rivard goes to a post office and uses a phone booth to call an individual named Flo. During the call, she confesses that she was instructed to lie about the scar and had previously accepted money to do so. Despite her fear of being an accessory to a crime or facing imprisonment, she agrees to meet Flo the following day because the offered sum of money is large enough to improve her life. She decides to proceed with the deception, planning to use excuses such as memory lapses or mistaken identity to evade police scrutiny.
